item.text: There isn’t an existing codebase here, so I’m treating the next goal as bootstrapping a small, complete project instead of guessing at missing files. I’m checking the runtime and then I’ll build one focused tool with tests and documentation.

Plan:
1. Define one concrete goal: build a seeded ASCII terrain generator CLI for the empty workspace.
2. Implement the generator and command-line entrypoint.
3. Add automated tests with the standard library so no external dependencies are required.
4. Document how to run it and verify the behavior locally.

item.text: I’m writing the initial project files now: a deterministic terrain generator, a CLI wrapper, and standard-library tests so the workspace becomes usable without any setup beyond Python itself.
The core implementation is in place. I’m running the CLI and the test suite now to catch any correctness or interface issues before I finish.

items.text: Define the project goal and structure
Implement a seeded ASCII terrain generator CLI

item.text:
I chose one concrete next goal for the empty workspace: turn it into a deterministic ASCII terrain generator CLI.

The implementation is in [terrain_cli.py](/workspace/terrain_cli.py), with standard-library tests in [test_terrain_cli.py](/workspace/test_terrain_cli.py) and usage notes in [README.md](/workspace/README.md). The tool generates seeded terrain maps so the same `--seed` always reproduces the same output, and it rejects invalid tiny dimensions.

Verification passed with `python3 -m unittest -v` (4 tests, all green). You can run it with `python3 terrain_cli.py --width 48 --height 18 --seed 42`.

result:
I chose one concrete next goal for the empty workspace: turn it into a deterministic ASCII terrain generator CLI.

The implementation is in [terrain_cli.py](/workspace/terrain_cli.py), with standard-library tests in [test_terrain_cli.py](/workspace/test_terrain_cli.py) and usage notes in [README.md](/workspace/README.md). The tool generates seeded terrain maps so the same `--seed` always reproduces the same output, and it rejects invalid tiny dimensions.

Verification passed with `python3 -m unittest -v` (4 tests, all green). You can run it with `python3 terrain_cli.py --width 48 --height 18 --seed 42`.
